title: Creepy Malt Greek Cream Flaky Pastry Pie
description: A classic galaktoboureko dessert featuring creamy galaktoboureko filling inside a flaky phyllo pastry and a hint of nutty flavor from ground nut oil.

ingredients.name: Malted milk powder
ingredients.notes: for adding a malty flavor to the galaktoboureko filling

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, sugar, and malted milk powder. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and vanilla extract. Temper the egg yolks by slowly pouring the warm milk mixture into the egg yolks, whisking constantly.
Return the saucepan to the stovetop and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the custard thickens, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the breadcrumbs and cinnamon. Let the mixture cool to room temperature.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the phyllo pastry to a large rectangle, about 1/4-inch thick.
Brush the top of the phyllo pastry with the ground nut oil.
Place a third of the phyllo pastry on the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Spread half of the galaktoboureko filling over the phyllo pastry.
Repeat the layering process, starting with the phyllo pastry, then the filling, and finally the phyllo pastry again.
Bake the galaktoboureko for 40-50 minutes, or until the phyllo pastry is golden brown and the filling is set.
Allow the galaktoboureko to cool in the pan for 10 minutes before serving.
